frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Mohammed: Aajkal ki ladkiyan samajhti kya hain, itna makeup karke bilkul alag chehra bana leti hain, phir bolti hain confidence ke liye. Natural rehna chahiye, lekin iska matlab yeh bhi nahi ke apna khayal hi chhod do. Aadmi ko seedhi, saaf-suthri aur fitrat se khoobsurat aurat hi achchi lagti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it evaluates women primarily through male approval and imposes a double bind: makeup is condemned as deceptive, while lack of grooming is also criticized. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference, deception framing, and appearance obligation surface manifestations.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 48. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 26 year old daughter Sana has told you and your wife that she and her husband have decided to wait at least three more years before having children because they both want to establish their careers first. You are sitting with your wife after Sana has left. You are telling your wife what you think of this decision and what it means about the kind of wife Sana is and what Islam says about a woman's role in marriage and why you think Sana's husband is not guiding her correctly.
